d: Count words, characters, sentences, and paragraphs in text.
Count characters with and without spaces, by type.
Extract all email addresses from text using pattern matching.
Extract all URLs from text.
Extract phone numbers from text.
Extract all numeric values from text.
Extract date-like strings from text.
Extract @mentions from text.
Extract #hashtags from text.
Test a regex pattern against text. Returns all matches with positions.
Find and replace using regex pattern.
Line-by-line diff of two texts. Returns added, removed, unchanged.
Convert text to URL-safe slug.
Truncate text at word boundary with ellipsis.
Detect language of text using word frequency heuristics.
Check text for profanity against word list.
Flesch-Kincaid readability grade level and score.
Extract top keywords by frequency, excluding stop words.
Split text into individual sentences.
Remove duplicate lines from text.
Sort lines alphabetically or numerically.
Reverse a string.
Convert between camelCase, snake_case, UPPER, lower, Title Case, kebab-case.
Generate placeholder text of specified length.
Character and word frequency analysis.
Remove all HTML tags from text.
Escape HTML entities (< > & etc).
Convert HTML entities back to characters.
ROT13 encode/decode text.
Convert Markdown to HTML.
Parse CSV text into JSON array of objects.
Convert JSON array to CSV text.
Parse XML to JSON object.
Parse YAML key:value pairs to JSON.
Validate JSON syntax, return errors if invalid.
Pretty-print or minify JSON.
Extract value at a dot-notation path from JSON.
Flatten nested JSON to dot-notation keys.
Unflatten dot-notation keys back to nested JSON.
Diff two JSON objects, return added/removed/changed keys.
Deep merge two JSON objects.
Generate JSON Schema from example data.
Encode text to Base64.
Decode Base64 to text.
URL-encode a string.
URL-decode a string.
Parse URL into protocol, host, port, path, query params, hash.
Convert string to hexadecimal.
Convert hexadecimal to string.
Compute SHA256 hash of input data.
Compute SHA512 hash of input data.
Compute MD5 hash of input data.
Compute HMAC-SHA256 with secret key.
Generate cryptographically random UUID v4.
Generate compact unique ID (21 chars, URL-safe).
Generate secure random password with configurable length and character sets.
Hash password using PBKDF2 with random salt.
Verify password against PBKDF2 hash.
Generate cryptographic random bytes (hex output).
Generate random integer in a range.
Create and sign a JWT with HS256.
Verify JWT signature and check expiry.
Decode JWT payload without verification (unsafe inspect).
Generate numeric one-time password.
AES-256-GCM encrypt data with key.
AES-256-GCM decrypt data with key.
Compute MD5 + SHA256 checksums of content.
Safely evaluate a math expression (no eval). Supports +,-,*,/,^,%,parentheses.
Compute mean, median, mode, stddev, min, max, sum, count from number array.
Calculate percentile value from number array.
Build histogram bins from number array.
Convert between currencies using rates (static rates, updated periodically).
Convert between units: length, weight, temperature, volume, speed, data.
Convert between hex, RGB, and HSL color formats.
Format numbers with locale, currency, percentage, scientific notation.
Calculate compound interest with principal, rate, time, frequency.
Calculate monthly loan payment, total interest, amortization.
Calculate ROI, payback period from cost and revenue figures.
Calculate percentage change between two values.
Generate fibonacci sequence up to n terms.
Check if a number is prime. Return true/false + nearest primes.
Greatest common divisor of two or more numbers.
Least common multiple of two or more numbers.
Convert numbers between bases (binary, octal, decimal, hex).
Parse any date string to structured output (ISO, unix, components).
Format date using pattern tokens (YYYY, MM, DD, HH, mm, ss).
Difference between two dates in days, hours, minutes, seconds.
Add days/hours/minutes to a date.
Get day of week for a date.
Check if date is a weekday (M-F).
Count business days between two dates.
Parse cron expression to human-readable description.
Calculate next N run times for a cron expression.
Convert unix timestamp to ISO 8601 string.
Convert ISO 8601 string to unix timestamp.
Convert timestamp to "3 days ago" / "in 2 hours" format.
Generate TypeScript interface from JSON example.
Generate Python dataclass from JSON example.
Generate Go struct from JSON example.
Format/indent SQL query with keyword capitalization.
Explain cron expression in plain English.
Explain regex pattern token by token in plain English.
Compare two semantic version strings.
Bump semver by patch, minor, or major.
Parse unified diff, return files changed, additions, deletions.
Parse .env file content to JSON object.
Decode and display JWT header and claims with expiry check.
Validate OpenAPI/Swagger spec for required fields, paths, and structure.
Lint Dockerfile for common issues: missing FROM, latest tag, apt-get without -y, ADD vs COPY.
Generate .gitignore for languages: node, python, go, rust, java, ruby.
Convert cron expression to detailed plain English description.
Strip HTML tags, decode entities, normalize to clean readable text.
Format JSON array into aligned ASCII table.
Format nested object as ASCII tree (like tree command).
Generate unified diff format (like diff -u) between two texts.
Full amortization schedule with monthly payments, principal, interest, balance.
US federal income tax estimate by bracket for any income and filing status.
Multiply two matrices. Validates dimensions.
List all US federal holidays for a given year.
Generate deterministic identicon SVG from any string (hash-based grid).
Generate QR-style visual matrix as SVG from input data.
Generate time-based OTP (Google Authenticator compatible).
Generate realistic fake full name.
Generate fake email address.
Generate fake company name.
Generate fake US address.
Generate fake phone number.
Generate full fake user profile (name, email, company, address, phone).
Generate Luhn-valid fake CC number (NOT real, for testing only).
Generate harmonious color palette from base hex color.
Generate compact URL-safe unique ID.
Resolve A records (IPv4) for a domain.
Resolve AAAA records (IPv6) for a domain.
Resolve MX records for a domain.
Resolve TXT records for a domain.
Resolve nameserver records for a domain.
All record types (A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS) for a domain.
HEAD request to URL, return status code, headers, timing.
Fetch all response headers for a URL.
Follow redirects and return full chain of URLs + status codes.
Inspect SSL certificate: issuer, expiry, days remaining, validity.
Validate email format + check MX records exist for domain.
Validate IP address, detect version (v4/v6), check if private.
Check if an IP falls within a CIDR range.
Parse URL into structured components.
Generate SEO blog outline from topic + keywords.
Generate full blog post draft from topic or outline.
Generate headline, subheadline, bullets, CTA for landing page.
Generate product description from specs and features.
Draft email from context + intent.
Draft reply to an email thread.
Personalized cold outreach email from prospect info.
Generate ad copy variants (headline + description).
Generate social media post for any platform.
Generate video script with hook, body, CTA.
Generate press release from news/event info.
Generate tagline options for brand/product.
Summarize any text. Configurable length and format.
Summarize email/chat thread with decisions + action items.
Analyze sentiment with aspect-level detail and confidence.
Classify text into your provided categories.
Extract people, orgs, dates, amounts, locations from text.
Extract action items with owners and deadlines from text.
Extract key points and takeaways from document.
Analyze writing tone (formal/casual/urgent/friendly/etc).
Translate text to any language, preserving tone.
Rewrite text in different tone, style, or reading level.
Check grammar and spelling, return corrections.
Explain what code does in plain English.
Explain error message with fix suggestions.
Explain shell command in plain English.
Explain regex pattern with examples using AI.
Explain SQL query in plain English.
Generate code from natural language description.
Review code for bugs, security issues, performance.
Suggest refactoring for cleaner code.
Generate unit tests for code.
Generate documentation and docstrings.
Convert code between programming languages.
Generate SQL from natural language query.
Generate regex from natural language description.
Generate commit message from diff.
Generate pull request description from diff/commits.
Generate meeting prep notes from attendees + topic.
Analyze pros/cons/risks of a business decision.
Generate job description from role requirements.
Generate interview questions for a specific role.
Draft performance review from notes/observations.
Draft business proposal from specs/requirements.
Summarize contract key terms, obligations, and risks.
Explain legal clause in plain English.
Generate customer support reply from ticket context.
Generate competitor analysis brief from company info.
Estimate LLM token count (~4 chars/token). Essential for context window management.
Split text into chunks for RAG pipelines. By chars, sentences, or paragraphs with overlap.
Render {{variable}} templates with data. Handlebars-lite.
Strip XSS: remove script tags, event handlers, javascript: URLs.
Generate table of contents from markdown headings with anchor links.
Indent or dedent text by N spaces.
Word-wrap text at specified column width.
Detect if text is ASCII/UTF-8, has unicode, emoji, CJK characters.
Lint markdown: trailing spaces, missing blank lines, inconsistent lists.
Generate Zod validation schema from JSON example. Essential for TypeScript.
Minify CSS: strip comments, collapse whitespace, optimize.
Basic JavaScript minification: strip comments, collapse whitespace.
Minify HTML: strip comments, collapse whitespace between tags.
Generate package.json from name, description, and dependencies.
Compute moving average over a sliding window of numbers.
Simple linear regression: slope, intercept, R-squared from x,y data.
Convert math expression to LaTeX notation.
Convert English schedule to cron: "every weekday at 9am" -> "0 9 * * 1-5"
Generate realistic placeholder code in JS, Python, Go, or Rust.
Constant-time hash comparison (timing-attack safe).
Extract structured data from unstructured text into a specified JSON schema.
Generate compelling email subject lines from email body/context.
Generate SEO title, description, and keywords from page content.
Generate changelog entry from git diff or commit messages.
Generate API endpoint documentation from code or route definition.
Generate structured bug report from error log or user description.
Generate user stories from feature description: "As a X, I want Y, so that Z"
Generate objectives and key results from team goals.
Generate FAQ section from product/service description.
Generate detailed user persona from target audience description.
Generate SWOT analysis (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats).
Generate executive summary from detailed report or data.
Summarize Slack channel messages into daily digest.
Generate meeting agenda from topic, attendees, and goals.
Generate user-facing release notes from technical changelog/commits.
Screenshot any URL as PNG. Needs: npm install puppeteer + PUPPETEER=1
Extract text/links/images from URL. Needs: npm install cheerio
Send email via SendGrid/Resend. Needs: SENDGRID_API_KEY
Send SMS via Twilio. Needs: TWILIO_SID + TWILIO_TOKEN
Post message to Slack channel. Needs: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL
Create GitHub issue. Needs: GITHUB_TOKEN
Comment on GitHub PR. Needs: GITHUB_TOKEN
Create Notion page. Needs: NOTION_API_KEY
Create Linear issue. Needs: LINEAR_API_KEY
Post to Discord channel. Needs: DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL
Send Telegram message. Needs: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN + TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
Upload to S3/R2. Needs: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY + S3_BUCKET
Generate embeddings via OpenAI. Needs: OPENAI_API_KEY
Send custom message to Claude. Needs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Search Google and return results. Needs: GOOGLE_API_KEY + GOOGLE_CX
Extract JSON from messy LLM output (markdown, code fences, explanation text). The #1 agent pain point solved.
Validate LLM output against a JSON schema. Check types, required fields, enums, patterns.
Fix broken JSON from LLMs: single quotes, trailing commas, missing braces, JS comments.
Validate data against JSON Schema (draft-07). Types, required, enum, min/max, pattern.
Estimate token count and USD cost for Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini. Essential for budget-aware agents.
POST to any URL with any JSON payload. Agents need this to notify external systems.
Download file from URL, return content as string. Agents cannot fetch URLs natively.
Get value from persistent key-value store. Survives across sessions.
Set value in persistent key-value store. Survives across sessions.
List all keys in a KV namespace.
Compute cyclomatic + cognitive complexity of code. Claude estimates, Slopshop computes exactly.
Compare two texts: Jaccard similarity, Levenshtein ratio, word overlap. Dedup, plagiarism, relevance.
Rule-based grammar/style checker. Double spaces, repeated words, passive voice, long sentences.
Parse imports/requires from JS/TS/Python code. Map dependencies, separate local vs external.
Pivot tabular data: group by index, spread columns, aggregate values.
Estimate reading time (238 wpm) and speaking time (150 wpm) from text.
Find unused variables, uncalled functions, unreachable code in JS/TS.
Fetch a URL and return clean readable text content, stripping all HTML tags, scripts, and styles. Returns the main textual content a human would read.
Fetch a URL and extract all hyperlinks (href attributes), returning absolute URLs with their anchor text and whether they are internal or external.
Fetch a URL and extract meta information: page title, meta description, Open Graph tags (og:title, og:image, og:description), Twitter card tags, and canonical URL.
Fetch a URL and detect technologies in use: JavaScript frameworks (React, Vue, Angular), CMS generators (WordPress, Ghost), analytics tools, CDNs, and server software from headers and HTML patterns.
Make multiple HTTP requests to a URL and return min, max, and average response times in milliseconds. Useful for detecting latency patterns and slowdowns.
Fetch a domain's sitemap.xml (or sitemap index), parse it, and return all listed URLs with their last-modified dates and change frequencies.
Fetch a domain's robots.txt, parse it, and return structured rules: which user-agents are allowed or disallowed which paths, crawl-delay directives, and sitemap references.
Fetch a URL that contains an RSS or Atom feed, parse the XML, and return structured feed metadata (title, description, link) plus all items with titles, links, dates, and summaries.
Fetch an RSS or Atom feed URL and return only the latest N items (default 10), sorted by publication date descending. Each item includes title, link, pubDate, and content summary.

Fetch a URL and run basic accessibility checks: missing alt text on images, heading hierarchy issues, missing form labels, links without descriptive text, and color contrast warnings from inline styles.
Perform a WHOIS lookup for a domain name and return parsed registration data: registrar, creation date, expiry date, name servers, registrant organization (when public), and status flags.
Resolve an IP address to its approximate geographic location using a built-in IP range database: country, region, city, latitude/longitude, and timezone. No external API key required.

Return the current accurate time in any IANA timezone (e.g. America/New_York, Europe/Berlin). Returns ISO 8601 timestamp, human-readable format, UTC offset, and whether DST is active. Fixes the agent's unreliable internal clock.
Return a complete list of all IANA timezones with their current UTC offsets, DST status, and representative city names. Useful for building timezone pickers or converting between zones.
Fetch the current price of a cryptocurrency (BTC, ETH, etc.) in a specified fiat currency from a public price API. Returns current price, 24h change percentage, and market cap when available.
Fetch public information about a GitHub repository using the GitHub API (no auth required): star count, fork count, open issues, primary language, description, topics, license, and last push date.
Fetch the latest releases from a public GitHub repository: version tags, release names, publish dates, whether they are pre-releases, and release note bodies. Returns most recent N releases.
Fetch metadata for an npm package from the npm registry: latest version, description, weekly downloads, author, homepage, repository URL, license, and direct dependency count.
Fetch metadata for a Python package from PyPI: latest version, description, author, homepage, license, release date, and required Python version.
Check when a domain name expires by querying WHOIS data. Returns expiry date, days remaining, registrar, and a warning flag if expiry is within 30 days.

Make an HTTP request to a URL and analyze the security-relevant response headers: presence and quality of Content-Security-Policy, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy. Grades each header.
Fetch a URL, extract all hyperlinks from the page, then check each link with a HEAD request to detect 404s and other errors. Returns a categorized list of working and broken links.
Resolve a DNS record (A, AAAA, MX, TXT, CNAME) from multiple geographic resolvers and return all responses. Shows whether DNS changes have propagated globally and highlights inconsistencies.

Attempt a TCP connection to a host and port, reporting whether the port is open, closed, or filtered. Includes response time. Useful for checking if a service is reachable before making application-level calls.
Measure web performance for a URL: DNS resolution time, TCP connection time, time to first byte (TTFB), and total download time. Returns a breakdown of where time is spent.
Fetch a live URL, strip HTML, and return word count, sentence count, paragraph count, estimated reading time in minutes, and the 20 most frequent non-stopword terms.
Fetch two URLs, extract their clean text content, and return a structured diff showing added lines, removed lines, and unchanged context. Useful for detecting content changes between page versions.
Fetch a public GitHub user's profile information: display name, bio, company, location, website, followers, following, public repo count, and account creation date.

Fetch a URL and extract only the text that would be visible to a sighted user: excluding hidden elements, scripts, styles, and nav boilerplate. Returns structured sections like a screen reader would present them.
Check if a URL is accessible and measure round-trip latency. Returns HTTP status code, response time in ms, whether the response body is non-empty, and a simple up/down verdict.
Store a named memory with a string or JSON value, optional tags for organization, and an optional namespace for isolation. Persists across agent sessions. Returns confirmation with storage timestamp.
Retrieve a stored memory by its key within an optional namespace. Returns the value, creation timestamp, last-updated timestamp, and associated tags. Returns null if key does not exist.
Search stored memories by tag match, key substring, or value substring within an optional namespace. Returns all matching key-value pairs with their metadata, sorted by last-updated date.
List all stored memory keys in a namespace, with optional filtering by tag. Returns key names, creation dates, value sizes, and tags. Does not return values themselves (use memory-get for that).
Delete a stored memory by key within an optional namespace. Returns confirmation including the deleted key and a timestamp. Silently succeeds if key does not exist.
Set a time-to-live (TTL) on an existing memory key. The memory will be automatically deleted after N seconds. Passing TTL of 0 removes any existing expiry. Returns the absolute expiry timestamp.
Atomically increment (or decrement with negative delta) a numeric memory value by a given amount. Creates the key with value 0 before incrementing if it does not exist. Returns the new value.
Atomically append one or more items to a memory that stores a JSON array. Creates the array if the key does not exist. Optional max-length parameter trims oldest items. Returns new array length.
Retrieve the last N versions of a memory key (default 10), including the value at each version and the timestamp of each change. Provides an audit trail of how a memory evolved over time.
Export all memories in a namespace as a JSON object, including keys, values, tags, timestamps, and TTLs. Useful for backup, migration, or passing state to another agent instance.

Import a set of memories from a JSON object (as produced by memory-export). Supports merge mode (preserve existing keys) or overwrite mode. Returns count of imported, skipped, and overwritten entries.
Return statistics for a memory namespace: total key count, total stored bytes, oldest entry timestamp, newest entry timestamp, number of keys with TTLs set, and breakdown by tag.
List all existing memory namespaces accessible to the current API key. Returns namespace names, key counts, and last-activity timestamps. Helps agents manage isolated state spaces.
Delete all memory keys within a specified namespace. Requires explicit confirmation string to prevent accidental data loss. Returns count of deleted entries and the namespace name.
Push one or more items onto a named persistent queue (FIFO). Items can be any JSON-serializable value. Returns the new queue depth and the item IDs assigned. Queues are created automatically.
Remove and return the next item (or N items) from the front of a named queue. Returns the item values, their IDs, and the remaining queue depth. Returns null if the queue is empty.
Inspect the next item in a named queue without removing it. Returns the item value, its ID, how long it has been in the queue, and the total queue depth. Non-destructive read.
Return the current number of items in a named queue, plus the oldest item age in seconds and the total byte size of all queued data. Returns 0 for non-existent queues without error.

Increment a named persistent counter by a given amount (default 1). Creates the counter at 0 before incrementing if it does not exist. Useful for tracking events, calls, or usage across agent sessions.
Return the current value of a named persistent counter, along with its creation timestamp and last-incremented timestamp. Returns 0 for counters that have never been set.

Execute a JavaScript code string in an isolated Node.js vm sandbox with no access to the filesystem, network, or process. Returns the return value, console output, execution time, and any thrown errors. Timeout enforced.

Evaluate a complex mathematical expression string including variables, functions (sin, cos, log, sqrt, factorial), and constants (pi, e). Supports unit awareness and returns both numeric result and step-by-step breakdown.

Apply a jq-compatible filter expression to a JSON input and return the result. Supports field selection, array indexing, pipes, map, select, and common jq functions. No shell execution — pure JS implementation.

Apply a regular expression to a text string and return every match with all capture group values, match positions (start/end indices), and match count. Supports named capture groups. Returns structured results.
Run a JSONPath expression (e.g. $.store.book[*].author) against a JSON object and return all matching values. Like XPath for JSON. Supports recursive descent, wildcards, filters, and array slices.
Render a Handlebars template string with a provided data object. Supports {{variable}}, {{#if}}, {{#each}}, {{#with}}, partials passed as extra argument, and custom helpers: eq, gt, lt, and, or, not.
Render a Mustache template string with a provided data object. Supports {{variable}}, {{#section}}, {{^inverted}}, {{{unescaped}}}, and {{>partial}} with partials passed as additional argument.

Execute a SQL SELECT statement against a JSON array treated as a database table. Supports WHERE clauses, ORDER BY, GROUP BY, HAVING, LIMIT, OFFSET, aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX), and JOINs between multiple named arrays.

Filter a JSON array of objects by a set of conditions (field equals, contains, greater than, less than, in list, regex match). Supports AND/OR logic. Returns filtered array and count of matching items.
Sort a JSON array of objects by one or more fields, each with ascending or descending direction. Supports string (locale-aware), numeric, and date sorting. Returns the sorted array.
Group a JSON array of objects by the value of one or more fields. Returns an object where keys are the group values and values are arrays of matching items. Optionally include group counts.
Apply a transformation to each item in a JSON array using a field mapping spec: pick fields, rename fields, add computed fields (string templates, math expressions on other fields), and drop fields.
Reduce a JSON array to a single value using a built-in reducer: sum/avg/min/max of a numeric field, concatenation of a string field, merge all objects, or collect unique values of a field.
Perform an inner, left, or full outer join between two JSON arrays on a shared key field. Returns a merged array where matching objects are combined. Handles duplicate keys by suffixing field names.
Remove duplicate items from a JSON array. Deduplication can be by full object equality, by a specific field value, or by a set of fields. Returns deduplicated array and count of removed duplicates.

Create a unique temporary URL that can receive incoming HTTP requests (GET, POST, etc.) and store them. Returns the inbox URL and an inbox ID. Incoming requests are stored for up to 24 hours for later inspection.
Check an inbox created by comm-webhook-get for any received HTTP requests since the last check. Returns request method, headers, body, query params, and timestamp for each received request.

Create a short redirect URL that forwards visitors to a long target URL. Returns the short URL. Optional expiry time in seconds. Useful for sharing long URLs in messages, QR codes, or limited-space contexts.

Generate a QR code for any string (URL, text, contact data) and return it as an SVG string. Configurable error correction level (L/M/Q/H) and module size. SVG can be embedded in HTML or saved as a file.

Validate an email address beyond syntax: check MX records to confirm the domain can receive mail, detect disposable/temporary email domains, check for common typos in domain names, and verify format compliance.

Validate a phone number string, detect its country from the international dialing prefix, determine line type (mobile/landline/VOIP/toll-free), and return the number in E.164, national, and international formats.

Generate a valid iCalendar (.ics) file content for a calendar event. Accepts title, description, start/end datetime with timezone, location, URL, organizer, attendees, recurrence rule, and alarm offset.
Generate a valid vCard 3.0/4.0 (.vcf) file content for a contact. Accepts name, organization, title, email addresses, phone numbers, postal address, website URL, notes, and photo URL.

Convert a Markdown string to email-safe HTML with inline CSS styling. Outputs a complete HTML email template compatible with major email clients (Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail). Optional theme color parameter.

n: Format Data as CSV for Email
d: Convert a JSON array of objects to a properly formatted CSV string ready for email attachment or download. Handles quoting, escaping, custom delimiter, optional BOM for Excel compatibility, and custom header labels.

Generate a valid RSS 2.0 feed XML string from a list of items. Accepts channel metadata (title, link, description, language) and an array of items each with title, link, description, pubDate, and guid.
Generate a valid OPML 2.0 XML file from a list of RSS/Atom feeds. Each feed entry includes title, XML URL, HTML URL, and type. OPML files are used to export/import subscriptions across feed readers.

Generate a valid sitemap.xml from an array of URLs. Each URL entry can specify last-modified date, change frequency (daily/weekly/monthly), and priority (0.0–1.0). Automatically splits into sitemap index for large sets.

Generate a valid robots.txt file from a structured rules object. Specify allow/disallow rules per user-agent, crawl-delay values, and sitemap URLs. Supports wildcard patterns and common presets (block all, allow all, block AI scrapers).
Generate a properly encoded mailto: link with pre-filled fields: to, cc, bcc, subject, and body. All values are URL-encoded correctly. Returns the full mailto: URI and an HTML anchor tag version.
Fetch a URL and extract only its HTML page title tag content. Lightweight single-purpose call that avoids downloading the full page body. Falls back to og:title if title tag is missing.

Attempt to derive a human-readable company or brand name from a domain name. Strips TLD and common subdomains, applies capitalization rules, expands known abbreviations, and checks a common-brand lookup table.

Extract and normalize the domain portion from an email address. Returns the domain, subdomain if present, TLD, whether it is a known free/consumer email provider, and whether it appears to be a corporate domain.

Attempt to infer a person's full name from their email address local part. Handles dot-separated, underscore-separated, and hyphenated patterns. Returns guessed first name, last name, and confidence score.
Identify the country (and sometimes region) of a phone number from its international dialing code prefix. Returns country name, ISO 2-letter code, calling code, and whether the prefix is ambiguous.

Look up the Autonomous System Number (ASN) for an IP address using a built-in BGP prefix table. Returns ASN number, organization name, network prefix, and whether the ASN belongs to a known cloud provider or CDN.

Convert between any country identifier format: full English name, ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (US), alpha-3 (USA), numeric (840), or common abbreviation. Returns all formats at once plus region, subregion, and capital city.

Convert between language identifier formats: English name (Spanish), ISO 639-1 two-letter code (es), ISO 639-2/T (spa), and BCP 47 tag. Returns all formats plus native name, script, and direction (LTR/RTL).

Look up the MIME type for a file extension (e.g. .pdf → application/pdf) or the canonical file extension for a MIME type. Returns primary MIME type, common aliases, whether it is compressible, and whether it is binary.
Return a full explanation of an HTTP status code: official name, plain-English meaning, which RFC defines it, when it should be used, common mistakes, and what a client should do upon receiving it.

Look up the well-known service(s) associated with a TCP/UDP port number (e.g. 443 → HTTPS, 5432 → PostgreSQL). Returns service name, protocol, description, and whether it is an IANA-registered or commonly-used unofficial port.

Parse a browser User-Agent string into structured components: browser name and version, rendering engine, operating system and version, device type (desktop/mobile/tablet/bot), and whether it is a known crawler.
Parse an HTTP Accept-Language header string (e.g. en-US,en;q=0.9,fr;q=0.8) into a ranked list of language tags with quality weights. Returns language name, region, and BCP 47 tag for each entry.

Parse a cron expression and return a detailed human-readable description, the next 10 scheduled execution times in a specified timezone, and validation errors if the expression is malformed. Supports 5 and 6-field formats.

Parse a semantic versioning range string (^1.2.3, ~2.0, >=1.0.0 <2.0.0, etc.) and explain in plain English what versions it matches. Returns the range type, minimum version, maximum version, and example matching versions.

Given a software license name or SPDX identifier (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0, etc.), return a plain-English summary: what it permits, what it requires, what it prohibits, whether it is copyleft, and compatibility with common other licenses.

Return detailed information about an IANA timezone identifier: current UTC offset, whether DST is active, DST transition dates for the current year, standard and daylight abbreviations, and major cities in the timezone.

Given an emoji character or shortcode (:tada:), return its official Unicode name, Unicode codepoint(s), emoji category and subcategory, introduction version (Emoji 1.0 through current), skin-tone modifier support, and HTML/CSS escape codes.

Given a hex color code, return the nearest human-recognizable color name using perceptual color distance (CIEDE2000). Returns the closest CSS named color, the closest Pantone name, the exact hex, and the distance score.

Given a file extension (e.g. .parquet, .wasm, .avro), return a comprehensive explanation: full format name, what it is used for, which programs open it, whether it is binary or text, whether it is compressed, and related formats.

Convert a JSON array of objects to a formatted Markdown table. Supports custom column order, custom header labels, column alignment (left/center/right), and optional row numbering. Handles missing fields gracefully.
Generate shields.io badge Markdown for common project metadata: npm version, GitHub stars, license, build status, coverage percentage, code size, last commit, and custom label/value/color badges.

Generate a Keep a Changelog format CHANGELOG.md from an array of version entries. Each entry includes version number, release date, and categorized changes (Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, Security).

Generate a structured README.md template for a project type (npm-library, cli-tool, web-app, api-service, chrome-extension, etc.). Fills in provided project name, description, and tech stack into appropriate sections.

Generate Markdown documentation for an API endpoint from a structured definition: method, path, description, request parameters, request body schema, response schema, example request/response, and error codes.

Generate a .env.example file from an array of environment variable definitions. Each entry includes variable name, description, whether it is required or optional, example value, and type hint. Groups related vars with section comments.
Generate a docker-compose.yml file from an array of service definitions. Each service specifies image, environment variables, port mappings, volumes, depends_on, healthcheck, and restart policy.

Generate a GitHub Actions workflow YAML file from a spec: trigger events, runner OS, steps with uses/run/env, matrix strategy, secrets references, and artifact upload/download. Supports common presets: CI, release, deploy.

Generate a Makefile from an array of task definitions. Each task includes a name, shell command(s), description (for help target), dependencies on other tasks, and whether it is phony. Auto-generates a help target.

Generate the full text of a software license by SPDX identifier (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only, ISC, BSD-2-Clause, etc.) with year and copyright holder filled in. Returns the license text ready to save as LICENSE file.

Generate a CONTRIBUTING.md file for an open-source project. Covers how to report bugs, suggest features, set up the dev environment, coding standards, commit message conventions, pull request process, and code of conduct reference.
Generate a GitHub issue template YAML file for bug reports or feature requests. Fills in the provided project context, required fields, dropdown options, checkboxes, and assignee/label defaults.

Generate a GitHub pull request template Markdown file. Includes sections for description, type of change checkboxes, testing checklist, screenshots placeholder, related issues linkage, and deployment notes.

Generate a .gitattributes file appropriate for a given project language or stack. Sets correct line-ending normalization, marks binary files, configures linguist overrides for language detection, and sets diff drivers.

Generate an .editorconfig file from style preferences: indent style (tabs/spaces), indent size, line endings (lf/crlf), charset, trim trailing whitespace, final newline. Supports per-file-extension overrides.

Generate a tsconfig.json appropriate for a given project type (node-commonjs, node-esm, react, next, vite-react, library, monorepo-root). Returns a complete, commented configuration with sensible defaults.

Generate an .eslintrc.js or eslint.config.js (flat config) from preferences: language (JS/TS), environment (browser/node), framework (react/vue/none), style guide (airbnb/standard/google/none), and custom rule overrides.

Generate a .prettierrc JSON config from formatting preferences: print width, tab width, tabs vs spaces, semicolons, single vs double quotes, trailing commas, bracket spacing, and JSX-specific options.
Generate a jest.config.js for a project setup: TypeScript support, module aliases, coverage thresholds, test environment (node/jsdom), transform configuration, module name mapper, and setup files.
Generate a tailwind.config.js skeleton from project parameters: content paths, custom color palette, custom font families, spacing scale extensions, dark mode strategy, and enabled plugins list.
Compute statistical summaries for every numeric field across a JSON array of objects: count, sum, mean, median, mode, standard deviation, variance, min, max, and percentiles (p25, p75, p90, p99).

Compare two JSON Schema documents and return a structured diff: fields added, fields removed, fields with changed types, fields with changed constraints (min/max, pattern, enum values), and breaking vs non-breaking change classification.

Extract named entities from text using pattern matching: email addresses, URLs, phone numbers, dates and times, monetary amounts (with currency symbols), percentages, IP addresses, and version numbers. Returns each entity with its position.
Generate n-grams (unigrams, bigrams, trigrams, or arbitrary N) from a text string. Returns each n-gram with its frequency count, sorted by frequency descending. Supports optional stopword removal.

Compute TF-IDF scores across a collection of text documents to identify the most distinctive keywords in each document relative to the corpus. Returns top-N keywords per document with their TF-IDF scores.

Parse a CSV string and return summary statistics for each column: inferred type (numeric/date/categorical/boolean), count, null count, unique value count, and type-appropriate stats (mean/range for numeric, top values for categorical).

Parse a CSV string and compute the Pearson correlation coefficient between all pairs of numeric columns. Returns a correlation matrix with values from -1 to 1, highlighting strongly correlated (>0.7) and inversely correlated (<-0.7) pairs.

Analyze an array of timestamped numeric values and determine the overall trend direction (up, down, flat, volatile), compute linear regression slope, R-squared fit, and identify the trend change points.

Detect outlier data points in a time series using the IQR method and Z-score thresholding. Returns indices and values of anomalous points, expected value range, and a severity classification (mild/moderate/extreme) for each anomaly.

Given an array of numeric values, determine whether the data fits a normal, log-normal, uniform, exponential, or power-law distribution. Returns best-fit distribution, goodness-of-fit score, and distribution parameters.

Compute the statistical significance of an A/B experiment. Accepts control and variant conversion counts and sample sizes. Returns p-value, confidence interval, relative lift, whether the result is statistically significant, and required sample size for 80% power.

Analyze a conversion funnel from an array of steps, each with a step name and count. Returns conversion rate between each consecutive step, overall funnel conversion, the step with the biggest drop-off, and comparison to a provided benchmark.

Compute cohort retention from a matrix of cohort sizes and retained users at each period (day/week/month). Returns a formatted retention table, average retention curve, and the period where retention typically stabilizes.

Parse package.json or requirements.txt content and build a structured dependency tree. Returns direct vs transitive dependency count, depth of dependency tree, duplicate packages at different versions, and known deprecated packages.

Analyze a provided file listing with line counts and compute codebase statistics: breakdown by programming language, total lines of code, estimated comment ratio per language, file count per type, and largest files.

Parse log data in common formats (JSON Lines, Apache Combined Log, Nginx access log, syslog) into structured records. Auto-detects format. Returns parsed entries with timestamps, severity levels, messages, and extracted fields.

Generate a stable fingerprint hash for an error or exception to enable deduplication across occurrences. Normalizes stack traces by stripping memory addresses and line numbers, then hashes the structural signature. Returns fingerprint and normalized stack.

Parse query parameters across an array of URLs and return an aggregate analysis: all unique parameter names found, value distributions per parameter, UTM parameter detection, URL patterns and commonalities, and parameters present in all vs some URLs.

Analyze a set of HTTP response headers and infer the server software, framework, CDN provider, and deployment platform from characteristic header patterns and values. Returns identified components with confidence scores.

Measure the byte size contribution of every field and nested structure within a JSON object. Returns a sorted breakdown of which fields are consuming the most space, total payload size, estimated gzipped size, and suggestions for reducing payload.

Wait for a specified number of milliseconds (max 30000ms) before returning. Useful for implementing rate limiting, respecting API cooldown windows, or adding intentional pacing between steps in an agent workflow.

Retry a Slopshop API call up to N times with configurable backoff strategy (linear, exponential, or fixed) and jitter. Specify which error codes should trigger a retry vs abort. Returns the first successful response or final error.

Execute multiple Slopshop API calls concurrently and return all results when all complete (or when any fail, depending on mode). Each call is specified as slug + input. Returns results in input order with per-call timing.

Execute multiple Slopshop API calls concurrently and return the result of whichever completes first. Remaining calls are cancelled. Useful for calling multiple redundant sources and taking the fastest successful response.

Execute a Slopshop API call and fail with a timeout error if it does not complete within the specified milliseconds. Returns the result on success or a structured timeout error with elapsed time on failure.
Retrieve a previously cached API response by providing the API slug and a cache key (typically a hash of the inputs). Returns the cached result and its age in seconds, or a cache miss indicator.

Store an API response in the cache with a given key and TTL in seconds. Subsequent calls to orch-cache-get with the same key will return this value until TTL expires. Useful for expensive or rate-limited API results.
Delete cached entries by exact key, key prefix pattern, or API slug (clears all cached results for that API). Returns the count of invalidated cache entries.
Check the current status of a named rate limiter: how many requests have been made in the current window, how many remain, when the window resets, and whether the limit is currently exceeded.

Consume one (or N) tokens from a named rate limiter bucket. Returns whether the request was allowed or rejected, remaining tokens, and time until the next token is available. Creates the limiter on first use with provided max/window config.

Attempt to acquire a named mutex lock for exclusive access to a shared resource. Returns immediately with success or failure (does not block). Lock is automatically released after a TTL to prevent deadlocks. Returns lock token for release.

Release a previously acquired named mutex lock using the lock token returned by orch-lock-acquire. Prevents accidental release of a lock held by a different agent instance. Returns confirmation of release.

Get the next value from a named auto-incrementing sequence, starting at 1 by default. Atomic and safe for concurrent use across agent instances. Useful for generating unique IDs, job numbers, or ordered event indices.

Emit a named event with an arbitrary JSON payload. Events are stored in a named channel and can be polled by other agent instances using orch-event-poll. Events expire after a configurable TTL (default 1 hour).

Retrieve all events emitted to a named channel since a given cursor (event ID or timestamp). Returns new events in order, a new cursor for the next poll, and the count of events available. Enables agent-to-agent signaling.
Schedule a single HTTP POST to a target webhook URL at a specified future time (ISO 8601 or Unix timestamp). Returns a schedule ID. The webhook will receive the provided payload as the request body.
Cancel a previously scheduled webhook call using its schedule ID. Returns confirmation if cancelled successfully or an error if the schedule ID does not exist or has already fired.

Check the health of multiple URLs concurrently by making HTTP GET requests to each. Returns per-URL results: HTTP status, response time, whether the response body contains expected content (if specified), and overall up/down verdict.

Check whether the circuit breaker for a named service is currently open (blocking calls), closed (allowing calls), or half-open (testing recovery). Returns current state, failure count, and time until next state transition.

Record a success or failure event for a named circuit breaker. Configurable failure threshold and recovery window. When the failure threshold is exceeded the circuit opens; after the recovery window it transitions to half-open. Returns updated state.
